name: Claude Computer Use
short_description: An API capability that lets Claude see a desktop screen and control mouse and keyboard — making any software interface a callable tool.
long_description: Announced in public beta on 22 October 2024, Claude Computer Use enables the model to perceive a live screenshot, then emit structured actions — mouse clicks, drags, keyboard strokes, and scrolls — to operate any desktop application or website. By treating the screen as both sensor and actuator, it collapses the distinction between "tools with APIs" and "tools with GUIs": every legacy app, web form, and file manager becomes reachable without custom integration. On OSWorld — a benchmark that scores screenshot-only desktop-task completion — Claude 3.5 Sonnet scored 14.9%, roughly double the next-best system at the time of release. The capability ships alongside bash and text-editor tools and is available on Anthropic API, Amazon Bedrock, and Google Cloud Vertex AI.
ai_note: Computer use is the capability itself — a large language model issuing structured GUI actions from vision input; AI is the entire mechanism.

images.caption: Claude Computer Use beta announcement — the model controlling a desktop environment via screenshot, click, and keyboard actions.
images.alt_text: A screenshot from the Anthropic blog showing Claude navigating a desktop application interface by reading the screen and issuing mouse and keyboard actions autonomously.

sources.title: Introducing computer use, a new Claude 3.5 Sonnet, and Claude 3.5 Haiku
sources.access_notes: Launch announcement, 22 October 2024. Describes OSWorld benchmark results and beta partners.

sources.title: Computer use (beta) — Anthropic documentation
sources.access_notes: Canonical technical reference: tool schema, security guidance, beta header values. Redirects to platform.claude.com.

interactions.title: Fill out a multi-step web form end to end
interactions.description: Given a goal like "submit a support ticket on this website," Claude takes a screenshot, locates the form, types into each field, handles dropdowns and date pickers, and submits — treating the browser as a tool with no custom API required.

interactions.title: Build and test a feature in a live IDE
interactions.description: Early-access partner Replit used the capability to let Claude open a code editor, write a function, switch to the terminal to run tests, read the output, and iterate — turning the entire development loop into a tool call.

interactions.title: Operate a legacy desktop app with no API
interactions.description: Because Computer Use works through the GUI rather than an integration layer, Claude can operate any installed application — a 2001-era accounting program, a proprietary CAD tool, or a government web portal — just as a human operator would.

interactions.title: Resist an injected instruction embedded in a web page
interactions.description: When Claude browses the web, malicious pages can embed prompt-injection instructions in text or images. Anthropic added on-path classifiers that detect suspected injections and pause to ask for human confirmation before proceeding — a documented safety mitigation.

interactions.title: Autonomously migrate data between two SaaS tools
interactions.description: Chain Computer Use with a bash tool: Claude logs into a source app, exports a CSV, opens the destination app, maps columns, and imports — a multi-app data-migration workflow that crosses app boundaries without any app-specific API credential.

interactions.title: Record and replay a repetitive workflow as a test
interactions.description: Ask Claude to perform a QA smoke-test sequence — open the app, click through the critical path, verify each screen — and capture whether each step succeeds, using its vision to confirm UI state rather than brittle CSS selectors.
